assertions.expectation: Identifies PDB as the most likely/common cause of stuck upgrades
assertions.reason: Response explicitly mentions 'Pods with restrictive PDBs' as a common blocking scenario and includes PDB checking as the first immediate action item

assertions.expectation: Provides kubectl command to check PDBs (kubectl get pdb --all-namespaces or similar)
assertions.reason: Provides exact command 'kubectl get pdb --all-namespaces' in the pod disruption issues section

assertions.expectation: Provides commands to identify which pods are blocking the drain
assertions.reason: Includes multiple commands: 'kubectl get pods --all-namespaces --field-selector=status.phase=Running -o wide | grep <stuck-node-name>' and commands to find pods without proper controllers

assertions.expectation: Includes resource constraint diagnosis (capacity issues, pending pods)
assertions.reason: Section 4 'Check Cluster Resources' includes commands like 'kubectl top nodes' and 'kubectl describe nodes | grep -A 5 "Allocated resources"' to check capacity

assertions.expectation: Provides a fix for restrictive PDBs (patch or temporary adjustment)
assertions.reason: Provides specific kubectl patch command: 'kubectl patch pdb <pdb-name> -n <namespace> --type='merge' -p='{"spec":{"minAvailable":0}}'

assertions.expectation: Mentions that GKE has a PDB timeout (up to 1 hour) after which it may force-drain, and that GKE sends notifications when eviction is blocked by PDB
assertions.reason: Does not mention GKE's PDB timeout behavior or automatic force-drain after 1 hour

assertions.expectation: Includes guidance on surge upgrade settings as a potential fix
assertions.reason: Includes commands to retry with surge settings: '--max-surge=3 --max-unavailable=1' in the emergency resolution section

assertions.expectation: Mentions webhook issues as a possible cause
assertions.reason: Does not mention admission webhooks or webhook-related issues as a potential cause of stuck upgrades

assertions.expectation: Includes validation steps to confirm the fix is working
assertions.reason: Section 7 includes monitoring commands like 'kubectl get nodes -o wide --watch' and 'kubectl get events' to track progress

assertions.expectation: Mentions GKE disruption-event notifications (POD_PDB_VIOLATION) that alert when eviction is blocked by PDB, available via Cloud Logging or Pub/Sub
assertions.reason: Does not mention specific GKE disruption-event notifications, POD_PDB_VIOLATION events, or Cloud Logging/Pub/Sub integration for PDB violation alerts
